completion_gates: every required workload artifact exists and passes its script-level checks
every hard-tail row reports status=exact and verified=true from state-only public-oracle input
distance-20 public benchmark shards have no timeout/failed rows under the approved runtime target
stronger H48 table campaign either produces trusted metadata or is explicitly removed from the claim
RubikOptimal hardcase rows either pass or remain documented as runtime-limit evidence
regenerated h48 oracle contract and thesis audit preserve all acceptance gates
only after the above can fast_runtime_proven_for_every_possible_state be revisited
hardtail_compute_efficiency_notes: Batch mode reduces duplicated process startup and table-load work by grouping public distance rows into fewer universal-oracle CLI invocations. The remaining hard limit is still exact-search node expansion on the unresolved rows; stronger heuristic tables are needed for a defensible 10x worst-case compute reduction.
notes: This is an execution plan, not completion evidence. It exists so cloud runs are reproducible, sharded, and tied to the thesis claim boundary. A canary-scope plan is useful for cost/risk screening, but only a full-scope plan can support the final every-state runtime proof gate.
objective: prove or falsify a fast optimal oracle claim for every valid 3x3 state

recommended_minimum_cloud_machine.reason: distance-20 hard-tail live exact search and h48h8 table generation are memory/I/O sensitive; the local 16 GiB Mac is not an appropriate proof machine under load

workloads.notes: Direct batch mode feeds multiple public distance rows into one universal-oracle CLI process. It preserves the exact/verified evidence rule while reusing resident native H48, Nissy, and RubikOptimal table-loaded sessions across the batch. This reduces duplicated process/table setup, but it does not by itself prove a 10x reduction in worst-case search-node work.
workloads.proves: live exact search over public known-distance hard-tail states with source sequences withheld from the public oracle
workloads.shell_command: env RUBIK_OPTIMAL_H48_THREADS=16 RUBIK_OPTIMAL_THREADS=16 python scripts/run_universal_oracle_cli.py --profile thesis --seed 2026 --solver h48h7 --no-random-cases --benchmark-distance 20 --benchmark-limit-per-distance 10 --benchmark-offset-per-distance 0 --timeout 600.0 --resident-h48-batch-timeout 600.0 --command-timeout 67200.0 --threads 16 --trusted-table --no-certificate-cache --universal-portfolio-prepass-timeout 60.0 --universal-portfolio-fallback-timeout 900.0 --universal-fallback-nissy-core-direct-timeout 300.0 --universal-resident-race-prepass-timeout 300.0 --universal-rubikoptimal-prepass-timeout 900.0 --universal-rubikoptimal-race-timeout 900.0 --universal-rubikoptimal-fallback-timeout 900.0 --universal-rubikoptimal-symmetry-variants 23 --universal-rubikoptimal-symmetry-timeout 900.0 --universal-rubikoptimal-symmetry-max-concurrency 4 --nissy-symmetry-variants 23 --nissy-symmetry-timeout 300.0 --nissy-core-direct-symmetry-variants 23 --nissy-core-direct-symmetry-timeout 300.0 --nissy-core-direct-symmetry-max-concurrency 4 --h48-parallel-symmetry-variants 23 --h48-parallel-symmetry-timeout 600.0 --h48-parallel-symmetry-max-concurrency 4 --symmetry-order-by-h48-lower-bound --symmetry-lower-bound-order-timeout 60.0 --h48-upper-bound-proof-timeout 600.0 --h48-upper-bound-proof-max-gap 4 --artifact-suffix cloud_hardtail_batch10_20260601_d20_o0_10

workloads.notes: This is the main route toward reducing worst-case H48 search depth variance.
workloads.proves: whether a stronger trusted H48 table can be generated and certified on the cloud machine
workloads.shell_command: env RUBIK_OPTIMAL_H48_THREADS=16 RUBIK_OPTIMAL_THREADS=16 python scripts/run_h48_stronger_table_campaign.py --profile thesis --seed 2026 --target-solver h48h8 --threads 16 --generation-timeout 21600.0 --certification-timeout 600.0 --runtime-target 300.0 --artifact-suffix cloud_hardtail_batch10_20260601_h48h8

workloads.notes: The local Mac timed this row out at 300s, so this is a cloud/idle-machine validation target.
workloads.proves: whether table-complete RubikOptimal clears the canonical distance-20 superflip hard state
workloads.shell_command: env RUBIK_OPTIMAL_H48_THREADS=16 RUBIK_OPTIMAL_THREADS=16 python scripts/run_rubikoptimal_oracle_corpus.py --profile thesis --seed 2026 --include-superflip --case-id superflip_distance_20 --timeout 900.0 --artifact-suffix cloud_hardtail_batch10_20260601_superflip
